evidence: "I wish it were longer, I would have liked to hear stories about all of the women killed."
"I found the whole story very sick and evil."
"The supposed plot twist was predictable. Ended up being silly."
"The story felt very detached, I didn't get a sense of who Mattie was or what he really cared about."
"I was especially touched by the work he did at the camp with the handicap children."
"I didn't care for this at all. The narration was awful, and the characters were all unsympathetic."
"Great acting, but the supposed plot twist was predictable."
"I was annoyed by the book, but I'm even more annoyed that people found it so fantastic."
"The only one who exhibited any emotion was Ms. Celia, and her story was just a sidenote."
"I found the whole story very sick and evil."
"It was all about him proving that he was 'normal'."

summary: The user shows a preference for drama and documentary genres but often finds narratives predictable and emotionally detached. They express a low sensitivity to production quality and a general dissatisfaction with character development and storytelling depth.
